source: Multi-Rate Deep Learning for Temporal Recommendation

concepts.finding: Multi-rate deep learning: models user preference evolution at multiple timescales. Slow-changing long-term preferences (months) + medium-term trends (weeks) + fast-changing short-term context (sessions). Separate recurrent networks per timescale with gated fusion. Outperforms single-timescale models by 8-12%.
concepts.implication: Multi-timescale keyword intent: long-term stable intent associations (e.g., "car" -> automotive) + medium-term trending intents (e.g., "AI" -> generative AI in 2025) + short-term session intents. Separate models per timescale capture different drift rates.
concepts.open_question: How rapidly do keyword-intent relationships change? Is a multi-timescale model necessary or does batch retraining suffice?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A multi-timescale keyword embedding model (slow + medium + fast components) adapts to intent shifts faster than single-timescale retraining, maintaining 5%+ higher accuracy during seasonal transitions.
